workflow_rationale: Single Rust service, straightforward implementation with clear ordered steps. No multi-service coordination or investigation required.

phases.name: Add Hash Dependencies
phases.description: Add blake3 or sha2+hex crate to bop-core for checksum computation

subtasks.notes: Per spec: check if sha2/hex already transitive, otherwise use blake3 (smaller, faster). Starting with blake3 as preferred option.

phases.name: JSONL WAL Implementation
phases.description: Implement append-only JSONL event logging to logs/events.jsonl

subtasks.description: Add append_event() function for JSONL WAL in lib.rs
subtasks.notes: Implemented Event struct and append_event() function with 5 unit tests. All tests pass. Function creates logs/events.jsonl with compact JSON lines, enforces 512-byte limit, uses O_APPEND for atomic writes.

subtasks.description: Call append_event in write_meta after tmp+rename

verification.expected: Existing write_meta tests pass, logs/events.jsonl created

subtasks.notes: Implemented: write_meta now calls append_event with meta_written event after successful persist(). Best-effort pattern (let _ =) used. Added test write_meta_appends_event_to_jsonl. All 102 tests pass.

phases.name: Checksum Field and Validation
phases.description: Add checksum field to Meta struct and implement validation in read_meta

subtasks.description: Add checksum field to Meta struct
subtasks.notes: Added checksum field to Meta struct as Option<String> with appropriate serde attributes for backward compatibility. Updated Default implementation. Compiles successfully.

verification.expected: Unit test verifies checksum written to meta.json

subtasks.notes: Implemented checksum computation in write_meta using blake3. The function now: 1) sets checksum=None, 2) serializes to compact JSON, 3) computes blake3 hash, 4) sets checksum field, 5) writes final compact JSON via tmp+rename. Added comprehensive test write_meta_checksum that verifies checksum is written, is valid hex, and matches recomputation. All 103 bop-core tests pass.

verification.expected: Unit test verifies checksum mismatch returns Err

subtasks.notes: Implemented checksum validation in read_meta. The function now: 1) reads meta.json, 2) extracts stored checksum, 3) sets checksum=None on a clone, 4) reserializes to compact JSON, 5) computes blake3 hash, 6) compares with stored checksum, 7) returns Err with warning message on mismatch. Added comprehensive test read_meta_checksum that verifies: valid checksum succeeds, corrupted checksum fails with appropriate error message. All 104 bop-core tests pass.

phases.name: Dispatcher WAL Integration
phases.description: Add stage_transition event logging to all 5 fs::rename calls in dispatcher

subtasks.description: Add stage_transition event logging at all fs::rename points

verification.expected: Existing dispatcher tests pass, events.jsonl contains stage_transition events

subtasks.notes: Added stage_transition event logging at all 5 fs::rename points in dispatcher.rs. Events are written to logs/events.jsonl in each card directory using append_event(). All writes are best-effort. Also fixed Meta initializers in cards.rs to include checksum field. Tests pass: cargo test -p bop --test dispatcher_harness (10/10).

phases.name: Documentation
phases.description: Write storage decision document and result summary

verification.instructions: Review docs/storage-decision.md for completeness: explains why filesystem+JSONL over SQLite/Dolt, covers ACID vs atomic rename, portability, zero-daemon philosophy

subtasks.notes: Completed: Created comprehensive 244-line document explaining bop's storage architecture decision. Covers: 1) Why filesystem state machine (fs::rename atomicity), 2) Why JSONL event log (append-only, human-readable, crash-safe), 3) Comparison with SQLite transactions, 4) Why not Dolt (duplicate versioning layer), 5) Zero-daemon philosophy, 6) When you WOULD want a database, 7) Scaling considerations. Follows same structure and style as format-storage-contract.md. Committed as 72ff9ff.

verification.instructions: Review output/result.md: documents corruption scenarios (adapter writes garbage, checksum mismatch) and mitigations (tmp+rename, checksum validation, JSONL audit trail)

subtasks.notes: Completed: Created comprehensive 385-line documentation covering 4 corruption scenarios (crash mid-write, direct overwrite, bit rot, race condition) and their mitigations. Documents: 1) tmp+rename atomicity from spec 022, 2) Blake3 checksum validation with canonical compact JSON, 3) JSONL event log for audit trail, 4) Recovery procedures via bop recover. Includes code examples, event log format, testing summary, and operations manual. Committed as 71f2aa4.

phases.name: Final Verification
phases.description: Run make check and verify all acceptance criteria

subtasks.description: Run make check and verify acceptance criteria

verification.expected: All tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean

subtasks.notes: COMPLETED: All acceptance criteria verified. Fixed orphan detection logic in bop clean to ignore events.jsonl (which is now always present due to write_meta). Make check passes: 373 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean. All deliverables confirmed: 1) logs/events.jsonl appended ✓ 2) WAL never aborts ✓ 3) meta.json has checksum ✓ 4) read_meta validates ✓ 5) docs/storage-decision.md exists ✓ 6) output/result.md exists ✓

parallel_groups.reason: WAL implementation and documentation have no dependencies and touch different files

parallelism.speedup_estimate: 1.2x faster with parallelism, but 1 worker recommended for clarity

acceptance_criteria: logs/events.jsonl appended on every meta write and stage transition
WAL write failure never aborts a state transition
meta.json has checksum field (blake3 of compact-JSON with checksum=None)
read_meta validates checksum and returns Err on mismatch
output/result.md documents corruption scenarios and mitigations
verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk: modifies core storage layer (read_meta/write_meta) but no external APIs. Requires unit test coverage for checksum logic.

acceptance_criteria_met: logs/events.jsonl appended on every meta write and stage transition
WAL write failure never aborts a state transition
meta.json has checksum field (blake3 of compact-JSON with checksum=None)
read_meta validates checksum and returns Err on mismatch
output/result.md documents corruption scenarios and mitigations
qa_signoff.notes: Excellent implementation quality. All acceptance criteria met. Comprehensive test coverage with 8 new unit tests. No security vulnerabilities. No regressions. Documentation is comprehensive (644 lines total). Ready for production.
